options.label: B

options.latex: Let F(x)=f(x).g(x). If f(x) is differentiable at x=a,f(a)=0 and g(x) is continuous at x=a then F(x) is always differentiable at x=a.

question_text: Let the functions f: (-1,1) -> and g:(-1,1) -> (-1,1)*be defined by f(x)=|2*x-1|+|2*x+1| and g(x)=x-[x], where [x] denotes the greatest integer less than or equal to x. Let fog:(-1,1) -> be the composite function defined by (fog)(x)=f(g(x)). Suppose c is the number of points in the interval (-1,1)*at which fog is NOT continuous, and suppose d is the number of points in the interval (-1,1)*at which fog is NOT differentiable. Then the value of c+d is _____ [JEE (Advanced)*2020] CD04-0484
